This includes, speaking in debates in the Chamber, asking Oral questions of Government Ministers, making contributions to the committee stages of legislation, and voting on the business of the day. Below, you can see just some of the ways I have been working for you in Parliament. If you want to keep in touch with all the work I am doing to represent you in Westminster and here in West Lancashire, you can sign up for my monthly newsletter. View my voting record Sign up to my monthly newsletter SEND Provision and Reform 13 Apr 2026 Commons debates I thank the hon. There is a balance to... Read more → Business of the House 05 Mar 2026 Commons debates Thank you, Mr Speaker; I am delighted to take up this place. People in West Lancashire are concerned about the potential relocation of the children’s accident and emergency department at... pages.title: Ashley calls for A&E services to be co-located at Ormskirk Hospital - Ashley Dalton - Ashley Dalton MP for West Lancashire
pages.h1: Ashley calls for A&E services to be co-located at Ormskirk Hospital
pages.meta_description: Official website of Ashley Dalton, Labour MP for West Lancashire
pages.text_excerpt: Ashley calls for A&E services to be co-located at Ormskirk Hospital - Ashley Dalton - Ashley Dalton MP for West Lancashire Skip to main content Join Get Involved Donate About Ashley My story My Plan for West Lancashire News Social Media Monthly Newsletter Local Media Latest News Parliamentary Work Campaigns Ashley fights for major investment in Skem – and wants your views Ashley calls for A&E services to be co-located at Ormskirk Hospital See the crack… report it back! SAVE THE CARE HOMES: We urge Lancashire County Council to keep our care homes and day centres open! Campaigns Won Sign my petition to save the War Memorial at Scarisbrick Village Hall! Contact Advice Surgeries Book an appointment Contact me Press Enquiries Privacy Policy Work with me Emergency and Support Services Acceptable Behaviour Policy Pride in Place Ashley fights for major investment in Skem – and wants your views More Menu 4th July, 2025 Ashley calls for A&E services to be co-located at Ormskirk Hospital Ashley at Ormskirk Hospital Last updated: 18 March 2026 We now know the results of the Shaping Care Together Joint Committee consultation: that A&E services will be co-located at Southport Hospital. I’m incredibly disappointed at this outcome and I know constituents across West Lancashire will feel the same way. I do not believe that this decision is in the best interests of the local community. Therefore, I have written to the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care to ask him to ‘call-in’ this decision. This means I am asking him to take a second look at this decision and consider reversing it. The independent report on the consultation clearly stated that “across nearly all questions, the Ormskirk option is viewed more positively by the overall respondent population”. I do not believe that A&E services being co-located at Southport is in the best interests of West Lancashire residents. My position has always been clear: Having spoken to many constituents about this issue as your Member of Parliament, I believe people in West Lancashire would be best served by A&E services being co-located and at Ormskirk Hospital. Frequently Asked Questions Who is making the decision on the future of A&E services? The decision was made by the Shaping Care Together Joint Committee and was announced in a public meeting on Friday 13 March. The Shaping Care Together Joint Committee is a committee of NHS Cheshire and Merseyside ICB and NHS Lancashire and South Cumbria ICB. The Committee is responsible for making key decisions for the Shaping Care Together programme. You were a Health Minister, were you able to secure the co-location of A&E services at Ormskirk Hospital? Until 2 March 2026, I was a Health Minister. Following the 13-week public consultation held last year, the decision on the future of A&E services in West Lancashire is being made by the Joint Committee of the Shaping Care Together Programme. There is no role for councillors, MPs, or ministers in this decision-making process. Government Ministers are not able to influence that decision, and it would not be appropriate for them to do so. This decision is for the SCTJC to make. As your MP for West Lancashire, and having spoken to constituents about what they want from local A&E services, I am strongly in favour of A&E services being co-located at Ormskirk Hospital. Throughout the consultation, I: Submitted a response to the consultation outlining that I am strongly in favour of A&E services being co-located at Ormskirk Hospital. Attended multiple meetings to represent the views of my constituents, based on what I have heard from them. Encouraged constituents to share their views and complete the consultation Posted about the consultation numerous times on social media to share updates and encourage constituents to attend meetings and respond to the consultation. Was interviewed on BBC Lancashire to share my views. Why did you not ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care to call this decision
